---
title: "Variants and Genomic Ranges"
subtitle: "Week 08"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-copy: true
---

## Purpose

Week 08 focuses on genomic coordinates: intervals, features, variants, and overlaps. These are the connective tissue of many genomics workflows.

## By the end of this week

You should be able to:

- Explain BED, GFF/GTF, and VCF at a high level.
- Use BEDtools-style thinking to intersect genomic features.
- Track genome builds and coordinate systems.
- Turn interval overlaps into interpretable biological summaries.
- Draft the ultimate figure for your project.

## Genomic intervals

Many questions can be expressed as interval operations:

- Which variants fall inside genes?
- Which methylation regions overlap promoters?
- Which peaks are near annotated features?
- Which eDNA ASVs map to reference sequences of interest?

The difficult part is often not the command. It is making sure the files use the same genome build, coordinate convention, and naming scheme.

## File formats

Common formats:

- BED: intervals.
- GFF/GTF: genome annotations.
- VCF: variants.
- BAM: alignments.

Each format has conventions. Read the header and keep an example row in your notes.

## Overlap is not meaning

An overlap is evidence, not a conclusion. You still need to decide what the feature means, whether the overlap is expected by chance, and whether the result supports the biological claim.

## Project checkpoint

Mock your ultimate figure. It does not have to contain final data yet, but it should show the layout, axes, comparisons, and caption logic.

## References

- BEDtools documentation
- Roberts Lab Handbook: Genomic Resources
- Bioinformatics Data Skills: working with genomic data
